body:
Following from #4627, I noticed that `enforce_open_boundary_mass_conservation!` was _always_ called and doing stuff even when there was no open boundaries.

This PR deals with that.

with @glwagner

labels.description: So we can get the wrong answer even faster
labels.name: performance 🏍️

title: Only enforce open boundary mass conservation for open boundaries

body: This PR allows using the `NonhydrostaticModel` on a `LatitudeLongitudeGrid`. We can perform some validation here before merging. with @francispoulin An example of a small hot box positioned at the bottom of a nonhydrostatic latitude-longitude grid leads to after about 10 minutes of evolution. The code is ```julia grid = LatitudeLongitudeGrid(size = (1, 100, 100), latitude = (-0.01, 0.01), longitude = (-10, 10), z = (-100, 0), topology=(Periodic, Bounded, Bounded)) model = NonhydrostaticModel(; grid, tracers = :b, buoyancy = BuoyancyTracer(), advection=WENO()) bₛ(x, y, z) = 2e-4 * 10 * (10 + 5 * z / 100) bb(x, y, z) = -90 < z < -70 ? (-0.005 < y < 0.005 ? 2e-4 * 10 * 5 : 0) : 0 bᵢ(x, y, z) = bₛ(x, y, z) + bb(x, y, z) set!(model, b=bᵢ) simulation = Simulation(model, Δt=10, stop_time=10minutes) conjure_time_step_wizard!(simulation, IterationInterval(10); cfl=0.5) run!(simulation) ```
title: `NonhydrostaticModel` on a `LatitudeLongitudeGrid`
